rationale: Y engages the two substantive GT threads that X misses entirely: the global-scope risk of making vllm_c default for every CUDA diffusion model (GT's line-252 concern), and it cites perf/accuracy numbers (26.9% Qwen-Image, -14.1% FLUX.1-dev, SSIM/PSNR figures) that match NumberWan's actual follow-up comments almost exactly, showing real grounding rather than fabrication. Both candidates independently note the vacuous CompilationMode parametrization in the new test, but Y extends this into a more actionable ROCm-divergence follow-up. X's two findings are accurate but trivial (suggesting explanatory comments), covering little of the reviewer's real concerns and missing the pytest-mark and scope-justification threads that GT emphasizes. Neither candidate flags the 'pytest mark not complete' inline comment, and neither includes a LATENT GAP CHECK section.
